description_long: This object is a metallic cartridge-shaped bomb, resembling a large caliber bullet with a cutaway section revealing its internal components. The exterior is primarily brass-colored with a rounded, copper-toned tip and a flat base, which is stamped with text. The cutaway reveals a dense, dark cylindrical core, likely representing explosive material, and a metallic primer at the base. The casing is smooth and reflective, with visible seams and a slight taper toward the tip. The overall construction suggests a compact, high-density device designed for detonation upon impact or via a primer mechanism.
description: A metallic, cartridge-shaped bomb with a brass casing, copper tip, and a visible cutaway showing internal explosive components.
description_view_0: Front view shows the flat, stamped base of the cartridge with the primer clearly visible and the cutaway section partially exposed.
description_view_1: Side cutaway view reveals the internal structure, including the dense explosive core and the primer at the base.
description_view_2: Tip view highlights the rounded, copper-colored projectile end and the partial cutaway along the side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displays the intact, smooth brass casing with the bullet tip and base visible, no internal components exposed.
attribute_detonation_mechanism: The object features a primer at the base, suggesting detonation is initiated by impact or pressure applied to the primer.
